feat: trie-based leader key system with SPC prefix bindings

Replace flat HashMap<KeyCombo, Action> with KeyTrie (Leaf/Node) in
layer bindings. Multi-key sequences like 'g g' and 'SPC f' are now
stored as trie subtrees and walked one key at a time.

Architecture:
- KeyTrie enum: Leaf(Action) | Node(HashMap<KeyCombo, KeyTrie>)
- Layer::bind_seq("space f", action) creates nested trie nodes
- KeymapSystem stores pending_trie when mid-sequence
- Escape or invalid key cancels pending sequence

Changes:
- Migrated g prefix from transient layer to trie (g g, g _)
- Added leader layer with SPC bindings (SPC f, SPC s, SPC v s, etc.)
- Leader layer active when vim mode is enabled
- Added goto-doc-start and goto-last-non-ws commands
- Added vault-switch and vault-open command dispatch from editor
- 131 tests passing (5 new trie/leader tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cascade): add retry, dependency-skip, and continue-after-failure to cascade loop

- Exception-only retry with exponential backoff (max 2 retries, 3s/6s)
- Dependency-aware skip: tasks depending on failed/skipped tasks are skipped
- Transitive dependency skip: if A fails, B skipped, C depends on B → also skipped
- Continue-after-failure: independent tasks still execute even if earlier tasks fail
- Cascade-level summary reply with succeeded/failed/skipped counts
- Only terminal attempt tracked in cascade steps (no retry pollution)
- 3 new tests: retry-on-exception, dependency-skip, transitive-skip
- Updated existing test: stops_on_failure → continues_after_failure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix(omada): Send auth duration in milliseconds

The Omada extPortal/auth endpoint expects the time
field as a duration in milliseconds.  The previous
fix sent seconds which the controller interpreted as
sub-second and expired the grant immediately.

Multiply the computed duration by 1000 so a 15-minute
voucher sends time=900000.

commit.message:
perf: replace 8m time_sleep with CRD poll for Crossplane Azure provider

The fixed 8-minute sleep was worst-case. The ProviderConfig CRD is
typically established within 1-2 minutes of the provider pod being ready.

Replace time_sleep.wait_for_azure_provider_crds with a null_resource that

feat: add possibility to amend configuration in a plugin (#5124)

We have a `preProcess` plugin that needs to store some information for
later use when a document is rendered. Currently, it is not possible to
update the `initialUserConfig` config object, since any update to the
`config` object is ignored. These updates in plugins are visible to
other plugins, but not to the rendered document.

There is a way to update the object with `"amend-user-config"`, but that
requires publishing to the internal system. That system isn't accessible
to a plugin.

commit.message:
fix: call test-associate after key exchange and handle broadcast messages (#2)

Three protocol-level fixes:

1. _ensure_session() now calls test_associate() after change_public_keys().
   KeePassXC resets its internal m_associated flag on every key exchange, so
   authenticated requests (get-logins, set-login, lock-database, etc.) all
   return error 8 (Association failed) unless test-associate is sent first.
   A new _associated bool tracks this per-session state.

2. _send_json() now uses JSONDecoder.raw_decode() instead of json.loads() to
   parse only the first JSON object from the recv buffer. KeePassXC sends an
   unsolicited 'database-locked' broadcast immediately after the lock-database
   response, producing concatenated JSON that json.loads() rejects with
   'Extra data'.

3. Error responses in _send_encrypted() are now logged at WARNING level
   (was DEBUG) so callers see the actual KeePassXC error message.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Copilot SDK Integration for Live Session Steering (#432)

* fix(sdk): remove auto-resume on view open — make steering opt-in

BREAKING: session.resume is no longer called automatically when opening
a session's conversation view. This was the root cause of all session
corruption issues — the auto-resume created a second CLI subprocess
(stdio mode) writing to the same events.jsonl.

Changes:
- Remove ensureSessionResumed() and auto-resume watcher
- Add explicit linkSession() function called only by user click
- Show 'Link for Steering' prompt with mode-specific explanations
- Command bar only appears after user explicitly links
- Action handlers (send, mode, model, abort) require isLinked
- Session label shows 'Unlink' instead of 'Stop'
- Add comprehensive 'No Auto-Resume' section to data flow docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(sdk): prevent schema mismatch on re-link + add unlink/destroy separation

Root cause: SDK subprocess writes 'attachments: null' while terminal CLI
writes 'attachments: []'. On re-link, new subprocess validates all events
and rejects null where Zod expects an array.

Fixes:
1. Always send attachments: Some(vec![]) instead of None in send_message
   — ensures events match CLI's expected schema
2. Add unlink_session (Rust → Tauri → TS → store → UI) that removes
   session from bridge map WITHOUT calling destroy — no shutdown event
   written, session stays alive in subprocess for safe re-link
3. 'Unlink' button now calls unlinkSession (keeps session alive)
   'Destroy' available for full teardown
4. Fix TCP mode messaging — TCP/ui-server IS for simultaneous terminal
   use, don't warn against it
5. Recommend --ui-server (TCP mode) in stdio mode link prompt

Full stack: BridgeManager::unlink_session → sdk_unlink_session command →
sdkUnlinkSession client → unlinkSession store → handleUnlinkSession Vue

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: unlink/destroy UI reactivity and --ui-server documentation

- Fix unlink not updating UI: add userLinked ref as explicit user-intent
  flag. isLinked = userLinked AND session.isActive. Unlink sets
  userLinked=false -> link prompt immediately reappears.
- Make unlink purely Vue state (no IPC call) — session stays alive in
  bridge for instant re-linking.
- Add separate Destroy button alongside Unlink for full cleanup.
- Reset userLinked on session navigation (watch on props.sessionId).
- Clear sentMessages on both unlink and destroy.
- Update --ui-server docs with verified findings: simultaneous TUI+TCP,
  multi-client protocol v3, exact startup instructions.

  and restarts). Auto-connect now uses saved CLI URL.

  subprocess via client.stop()).

  explains how to find --ui-server port.

  on page navigation).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace platform-specific WI_* symbols with the new ax* / AX_* names across the codebase. * Fix Windows test crash: skip paint messages in repost_messages() when !running

create_window() calls invalidate_window() which posts kWindowMessagePaint
and kWindowMessageNonClientPaint. When repost_messages() dispatches these
to window procs (e.g. win_button), they call fill_rect()/draw_text_small()
which use OpenGL function pointers. On Windows without glewInit() these
pointers are NULL, causing a crash with no output.

commit.message:
fix: preserve key order in YAML output (#2034)

* fix: preserve key order in YAML output

MlrmapToYAMLNative converted the ordered Mlrmap to map[string]interface{},
which loses insertion order. yaml.v3.Marshal then sorts Go map keys
alphabetically, so YAML output always had sorted keys despite other formats
(JSON, pretty-print) correctly preserving order.

Switch to building yaml.Node trees that preserve the Mlrmap's linked-list
iteration order. Use yaml.Node.Encode for scalar values to correctly handle
edge cases (NaN/Inf, non-UTF-8 strings).

Fixes #2028.

* feat: §29.8.5 cache keying by project (#265)

Add project-scoped state caching for modules to prevent cross-project
data leakage. Module state (list data, filters, scroll positions, etc.)
is keyed by moduleId+projectId and automatically switches when the
active project changes.

New SDK hook: useProjectScopedState(key, initialValue) — works like
useState but the value is keyed by moduleId+projectId+key. Previous
project's cached values are preserved for instant restore on switch-back.

Implementation uses nested Map<moduleId, Map<projectId, Map<key, value>>>
to avoid composite-string key pitfalls. Cache entries are cleaned up on
module dispose (both inline and via registry._disposeSuspended()).

which is volume-mounted as matrix-keys in docker-compose.yml.
Includes a fallback guard for edge cases where UserProfile is empty.

the-matrix_matrix-keys volume into the active docker_matrix-keys
volume, so existing encrypted secrets will decrypt again after
container restart.

* fix: pin Docker Compose project name to prevent volume splitting

Docker Compose derives the project name from the directory name by default.
Running `docker compose` from different directories (e.g. `docker/` vs a
deployment dir) created separate named volumes, causing the OAuth config
stored in SQLite to be stranded in one volume while the app ran against a
different empty volume — prompting the setup wizard on every restart.

Changes:
- Add `name: the-matrix` to all three compose files so the project name
  is stable regardless of which directory `docker compose` runs from
- Add automatic volume migration to update.sh that detects orphaned
  volumes from previous project names and copies data + encryption keys
- Override still possible via COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME or `-p` flag

Replace the no-op DiscordClientAdapter with a real implementation
wrapping Discord.Net's DiscordSocketClient. All IDiscordClient methods
now perform actual Discord API calls:

- Gateway connect/disconnect with Ready event awaiting
- Message send (text and embed) to channels

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use unique DB names in SqliteAuditLog and SystemConfig tests (#284)

Hardcoded 'Data Source=AuditLogTest' and 'Data Source=SystemConfigTest'
caused cross-test interference during parallel xUnit execution. All other
Sqlite test classes already used Guid-based unique names.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: break DI cycle causing startup deadlock (#287)

PluginHost resolved ISessionManager eagerly, creating a circular
dependency: ISessionManager → CommandRouter → AgentCommandHandler →
IMultiAgentSessionManager → ISessionManager. Wrapping in Lazy<>
defers resolution until first plugin activation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: create session-resumed TCS before Spawning state transition (#288)

The _sessionResumedTcs was created AFTER TransitionState(Spawning),
creating a race where HandleSessionResumedAsync could observe Spawning
state but find a null TCS — silently dropping the resume signal and
causing a 30s timeout to Error state.

Move TCS creation before the state transition so it is always available
when HandleSessionResumedAsync checks _state == Spawning.

Fixes FullCompactionFlow_Success test flake.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: throttle xUnit parallel threads to eliminate flaky tests (#289)

Add xunit.runner.json with maxParallelThreads=2 to prevent thread pool
saturation that caused random test failures under full parallelism (16
threads on this machine).

Before: ~3 random failures per run (different tests each time —
SqliteAuditLog, PluginFileWatcher, SessionOperator, BoundedLineBuffer)
After: 0 failures across 3 consecutive runs (2906/2906 pass)

Tradeoff: test suite runs ~70s instead of ~35s. Worth it for CI
reliability.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove duplicate CliEventBroadcasterTests with race-prone Task.Run pattern (#290) (#292)

CliEventBroadcasterTests duplicated ChannelCliEventBroadcasterTests but used
Task.Run + CancellationTokenSource which flakes under parallel execution.
The ChannelCliEventBroadcasterTests cover the same behavior using the safer
publish-then-subscribe pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use ConcurrentQueue for thread-safe state change tracking in SLM tests (#293)

SessionLifecycleManagerTests used List<T> for _stateChanges which was written
from the monitoring loop thread (event handler) and read from the test thread
(WaitForState polling). List<T> is not thread-safe — concurrent Add + LINQ
enumeration can corrupt state or throw. Replaced with ConcurrentQueue<T>
which safely supports concurrent Enqueue + enumeration.

Also removed duplicate CliEventBroadcasterTests.cs (already merged on develop
via #292).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add Enable/Disable buttons to plugin settings UI (#294)

The Plugins tab in Settings displayed plugin state (Discovered/Active/Faulted)
but had no way to actually enable or disable a plugin. The enablePlugin and
disablePlugin API functions existed in the store and API client but were never
wired to UI controls.

This meant the Discord plugin (and any future plugin) would stay in Discovered
state forever because users had no way to activate it through the UI.

Changes:
- Add Enable/Disable toggle button on each plugin card in Settings > Plugins
- Add 'Save & Enable' button in plugin settings form for inactive plugins
- Show error message when a plugin is in Faulted state with lastErrorMessage
- Add pluginToggle CSS class for the toggle buttons
- Wire buttons to existing pluginStore.enablePlugin/disablePlugin actions
- Refresh plugin list after enable/disable operations

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: store plugin settings as plain text instead of JSON-serializing strings (#295)

The PluginController was wrapping string values in JSON serialization before
storage (e.g., 'hello' → '"hello"' in DB), then unwrapping on read. This
caused quotation marks to accumulate in the UI after save→read→save cycles
because the unwrap was fragile and the response fed back into the next save.

Changes:
- PluginController: store non-secret string/select values as plain text,
  matching ModuleController behavior. Numbers and booleans unchanged.
- PluginSettingsAdapter.Get<T>(): special-case string to unwrap legacy
  JSON-quoted values while returning new plain-text values directly.
  Non-string types still use JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>() with a
  fallback on JsonException.
- SqlitePluginSettingsStore.UnwrapJsonString(): loop to strip multiple
  layers of accumulated quoting (self-healing for corrupted DB values).
- Tests updated: 4 new tests for plain-text storage, legacy compat,
  and multi-layer unwrap. Existing roundtrip test updated.

Closes the recurring plugin settings quoting bug.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: wire session monitoring gauges to CompactionMetrics instruments

RecordGaugeMetrics() now writes context_usage_percent, message_count,
and uptime_seconds to the CompactionMetrics gauge instruments every
5 seconds from the monitoring loop. Values come from the same internal
fields used by GetCurrentMetrics().

Adds MeterListener-based test verifying all three gauges are recorded
with correct values.

Updates spec §8.12 and §8.13 observability notes to reflect
implementation status.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add model usage enrichment API endpoints (§27.7.3, §27.7.4) (#298)

Add PUT /api/sessions/{id}/metrics/model-usage for single-session import
and PUT /api/sessions/metrics/model-usage/batch for bulk import of
per-model token usage data from external tools like operator-ingest.py.

This closes the write path gap identified in issue #219 — the schema and
read path existed but had no way to ingest data.

Includes:
- Input validation (non-negative counters via [Range], empty model list rejection)
- Owner-scoped access (sessions not owned by caller are skipped/404'd)
- 8 unit tests covering all error paths and happy paths
- Frontend API client functions (importModelUsage, batchImportModelUsage)
- Spec §27.7.3 and §27.7.4 documenting the new endpoints

Closes #219

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace fixed delay with polling loop in MonitoringLoop_RecordsGaugeMetrics test

The test used a fixed 500ms Task.Delay which was flaky under heavy parallel
test load — the MeterListener subscription might not be fully active in time.
Replace with a polling loop (50ms intervals, 3s deadline) that calls
RecordObservableInstruments() until all expected gauge keys are present.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use REST API fallback for guild membership check during startup

GetGuildInfoAsync relied on the DiscordSocketClient's in-memory cache,
but during OnStartupAsync the socket client hasn't connected yet (that
happens in ExecuteAsync). The empty cache caused the method to always
return null, producing a false 'Bot is not a member of server' error.

Fix: when the socket cache returns null, fall back to the Discord REST
API to verify guild membership. Also:
- Only return null for HTTP 404/403 (actual not-a-member); propagate
  other exceptions so transient failures aren't misreported
- Skip permission warnings when permissions are unknown (REST path);
  they'll be validated once the gateway connects
- Store bot token for REST fallback reuse
- Use CancellationToken via WaitAsync on the REST call

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add startup announcement and channel creation on Discord connect

The bot previously connected silently with no visible feedback — no channel
created, no message posted. Users enabling the plugin mid-session or for the
first time saw nothing in their Discord server.

Now on first gateway connect:
- Creates the default orchestrator channel (matrix-default/orchestrator)
- Posts a welcome embed with available slash commands
- Detects and announces any active agent sessions (handles mid-session setup)

Also improved FormatConnected to show actionable info: what the channel does,
how to interact, and available commands.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent task runner timeout from killing Discord gateway

The PluginBackgroundTaskRunner applies a 30s per-tick timeout to
ExecuteAsync. The gateway's Task.Delay(Infinite) was getting cancelled
after 30s, killing the connection permanently (circuit breaker tripped).

Fix: block on PluginStopping token instead of the runner's tick token.
PluginStopping only fires on plugin deactivation, keeping the gateway
alive indefinitely. Updated tests to provide a cancellable PluginStopping
token.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: auto-create sessions from Discord and remove TTL setting

When a Discord message arrives and there's no running session, the plugin
now creates one automatically (instead of returning 'No active session').
Only users listed in authorizedApprovers can trigger session creation.
A SemaphoreSlim prevents duplicate sessions from concurrent messages.

Also:
- Treat Idle sessions as deliverable (not just Running)
- Add CreateSessionAsync to IPluginSessionApi (gated behind sessions:write)
- Remove defaultChannelTtlMinutes from plugin settings UI
- Pass AgentSettings and workspace root to PluginHost for session creation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use real user ID for Discord-created sessions

The sessions table has a FK constraint on user_id → users(user_id).
Using a synthetic 'discord-plugin' user ID caused SQLite Error 19.
Now looks up the most recent session's owner as the session creator.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: wire plugin observer pipeline to session events

The PluginObserverPipeline existed but nobody called it — agent output
was never forwarded to plugin observers like DiscordObserver. Created
PluginObserverBridgeService that:
- Subscribes to SessionCreated/Terminated domain events
- Intercepts CLI events from BroadcastingSessionEventSink
- Buffers AgentMessageChunkEvents until IsFinal, then sends complete
  message to observers
- Forwards tool calls and system commands to observers

This completes the bidirectional Discord bridge: messages go in via
DiscordGatewayTask and responses come back via DiscordObserver.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove per-tick timeout that killed Discord gateway after 30 seconds

The PluginBackgroundTaskRunner applied a 30-second handler timeout to
ExecuteAsync via CancellationTokenSource.CancelAfter. This killed the
Discord gateway task 30 seconds after startup — only the startup
announcement got through before the task died.

The previous attempt to fix this (using PluginStopping token) didn't
work because the tickCts cancellation propagated through the
cancellationToken parameter regardless.

Root fix: ExecuteAsync now receives the plain shutdown token with no
per-tick timeout. Background tasks like the Discord gateway run for
their natural lifetime. The circuit breaker still handles crashes.

Also moves slash command registration from OnStartupAsync to
ExecuteAsync (after ConnectAsync), because RegisterSlashCommandsAsync
needs an active gateway connection to resolve the guild.

events beyond its 500-event capacity. Now the onreconnected handler
also fetches the full persisted event history from the API and rebuilds

history to prevent stale RAF-buffered chunks from appending after
the replacement.

Adds tests for reconnect history reload and error resilience.

causing Link-navigation to serve stale prefetched RSC payloads for up
to 5 minutes after server-side data changes (stock, images, prices).
Full reloads were fresh because they bypass the client Router Cache.

30s is the minimum Next.js allows and gives a good balance: prefetch
still works so navigation feels instant, but stale data windows shrink
from 5 min to 30 s — acceptable for a marketplace where product data
(images, stock, prices) changes frequently.

"Add to cart" text to be cut off. Moved to a floating overlay on the
top-right of the product image — consistent with the favorites page
pattern and standard e-commerce UX.

* feat: warn about insufficient stock in cart before checkout

Adds an inline stock check on the cart page so buyers see issues
before reaching checkout: per-item warning badges, "adjust to
available" / "remove" actions, a banner when any line is affected,
and a disabled checkout button until all warnings are resolved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: pluralize cart stock 'only N left' message

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: parallelize jobs and cache deps for faster pipeline

- All three jobs (verify/build/integration) now run in parallel; the
  needs: chain that gated integration behind verify+build is gone (no
  shared artifacts anyway).
- New composite action setup-deps caches node_modules + Prisma client
  keyed on package-lock + schema, so npm ci/prisma generate are skipped
  on cache hit.
- Verify runs typecheck:app, typecheck:test, and the test suite in
  parallel inside a single step, with grouped logs on completion.
- PRs run plain test:parallel; coverage only runs on main pushes.
- Build job now caches .next/cache between runs.
- DB-backed tests use test:db:parallel (script switched to
  --env-file-if-exists so it works in CI without a pre-created .env).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: always run prisma generate (Prisma 7 outputs outside node_modules)

Prisma 7 generates the client into src/generated/prisma, which lives
outside the cached node_modules path. Skipping prisma generate on a
cache hit left the generated client missing, causing every Prisma type
to collapse to {} and ~500 typecheck errors.

Always run prisma generate (it's ~2s) and drop schema.prisma from the
cache key since it no longer affects what's cached.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- SignOutButton: replace hardcoded "Cerrar sesion" with t('signOut') so the

- buyerAccountItems: mark /cuenta/perfil as available — the page and form
  already exist, but the entry was stuck on "Coming soon", hiding the
  personal data section behind a disabled placeholder
